Chart of Accounts Purchasing Invoicing Expenses Reconciliation Financial Statements Dashboard
Monicah Mburu · Finance & Operations

Bookkeeping with QuickBooks Online

Techmate Solutions Ltd.

A simulated computer hardware retailer, built in QuickBooks Online to demonstrate a full bookkeeping cycle from creating the chart of accounts to reconciled financial statements the way I'd run it for a real client.

Platform
QuickBooks Online Plus
Reporting period
1 Jan – 6 Jul 2026
Industry
Computer hardware retail (simulated)
Currency
Kenya Shillings (Ksh)
02
Project Overview

Why I built it this way

A full bookkeeping cycle, including the untidy middle: live bank feed, low stock inventory, overdue invoices, automated receipts.

Techmate Solutions Ltd. is a fictional Nairobi computer retailer, built in QuickBooks Online I handled converting purchase orders into inventory, invoices into Accounts Receivable, a Google Sheets → Zapier → Expensify expense pipeline, bank feed rules, and monthly reconciliation.

Chart of Accounts Inventory & COGS Sales & Invoicing Accounts Receivables Monitoring Vendor Bills & POs Bank Feed Rules Bank Reconciliation Expense Automation Financial Reporting
Ksh27.56M
Total income recorded, 1 Jan – 6 Jul 2026
Ksh6.05M
Net earnings which ties exactly to Net Income on the Balance Sheet
Ksh0.10
Reconciliation variance across 129 payments & 26 deposits
03
Foundations

Chart of accounts

Built to match how a computer retailer operates.

I rebuilt the default category list to match Techmate's actual costs. I separated Cost of Sales from operating expenses, created specific expense accounts like Equipment Maintenance, Hosting, Website Maintenance, Shipping Fees and Transport Cost as well as organized current assets into detailed categories

Skills demonstrated
Account structuring COGS setup Detailed Expense Mapping Accrual basis configuration
04
Operations

Customers, vendors & inventory

Inventory updates only when goods are received, not when purchase orders are created.

The business has three product tiers; Business, Workstation and Gaming, each tracked with quantity on hand and automatic reorder flags. The endor side runs a three way match beginning when the PO is raised, then the item receipt is logged on arrival and finally the bill is entered against the receipt which makes sure that Inventory Asset never gets ahead of what's on the shelf.

Skills demonstrated
Inventory tracking Reorder point monitoring 3 way matching (PO → receipt → bill)
05
Revenue Cycle

Sales & invoicing

Ksh27.56M in income over the period, tracked from invoice to deposit.

Every sale ran through a proper invoice, not a sales receipt shortcut. As of this snapshot, everything unpaid had aged into overdue which is an early warning sign the business is growing revenue faster than it's collecting it.

Skills demonstrated
Invoice creation Accounts Receivables monitoring Deposit tracking Revenue recognition
06
Automation

Expenses & vendor bills

Field receipts reach the ledger without anyone retyping them.

A two step Zap watches a shared Google Sheet for new receipt rows and creates the matching expense in Expensify automatically. Expensify's native QuickBooks Online connection then syncs the finished report into the ledger.

1
Google Sheets
Field staff log receipt: date, merchant, category, amount
→
2
Zapier
New/updated row triggers "Create Single Expense" and the Zap ends here
→
3
Expensify
Expense lands on the open report, categorized
→
4
QuickBooks Online
Synced via Expensify's native QBO connection, outside the Zap
Here I traced the numbers and did not assume they tie out
Fuel expense: 3 receipts in Expensify vs. P&L Ksh 14,700.00 both ✓
Furniture and Fixtures: 1 receipt (office furniture upgrade) vs. P&L Ksh 300,000.00 both ✓
Equipment Maintenance: 6 receipts in Expensify total Ksh 41,900.00, but P&L shows Ksh 38,400.00

Why the Ksh 3,500.00 gap exists: One repair receipt dated 3 August falls after the 6 July reporting cutoff. The P&L correctly excludes it because it records transactions by date of occurrence, not capture date. This is proper accrual accounting.

Skills demonstrated
Zapier automation Receipt to ledger pipeline Bank rule configuration Source to report tracing

Accounts Payable aging as of July 7, 2026

Vendor balances broken down by how long the invoices have been outstanding. A/P is far smaller than A/R in this case, though both open balances have aged into the 91+ bucket.

Accounts Payable vs. Accounts Receivable contrast
Two vendors, Ksh858K owed, both 91+ days out but manageable given the low total. When we compare this to Accounts Receiveble ,nine customers owe Ksh15.1M with Ksh14.4M in the same 91+ bucket. The payables are small and controllable, the receivables are the problem.
07
Controls

Bank reconciliation

From 125 pending transactions to a reconciled account with a documented variance.

Matched transactions QuickBooks could confidently pair, resolved ambiguous ones by hand, and let rules handle recurring categories. Reconciliation closed with a ten cent variance rather than a forced zero.

About the screenshots in this section
Shown to document the mechanics of the process from matching, rule creation and closing a cycle.
Reconciliation summary for Cash at BankAmount (Ksh)
Statement ending date1 February 2027
Beginning balance0.00
Cheques and payments cleared (129)–51,586,681.10
Deposits and other credits cleared (26)28,884,985.00
Statement ending balance–22,701,696.00
Difference (left unadjusted)
Skills demonstrated
Bank feed matching Rule automation Monthly reconciliation Variance investigation & materiality judgment
08
Receivables

Accounts Receivables aging

Customer balances by aging bucket where payment accountability sits.

Pulled directly from QBO as of July 7, 2026. The aging tells the real story. Every customer except POS Client is 91+ days overdue; POS Client alone sits in the 31-60 range. Total Accounts Receivable of Ksh15.15M with Ksh14.41M sitting in the 91+ bucket signals serious collection pressure.

What this shows
Only one customer (POS Client) sits in the 31-60 day bucket; every other customer is 91+ days past invoice. Net 30 terms mean this is well overdue. The aging signals a collection gap invoices are being issued, but money isn't flowing back fast enough.
Skills demonstrated
Accounts Receivable monitoring and reporting Aging bucket analysis Customer credit management
09
Close

Financial statements

Accrual basis, period ended 6 July 2026 fully tied out.

Net Earnings on the P&L matches Net Income on the Balance Sheet exactly; Total Assets equals Total Liabilities & Shareholders' Equity to the cent. Every subtotal is copied directly from QuickBooks.

Skills demonstrated
P&L /Income Statement preparation Balance Sheet preparation Accrual basis reporting Statement verification
10
Day to day

The view a business owner actually sees

The dashboard a founder checks between board meetings.

QuickBooks' "Business at a glance" dashboard Profit &Loss trend, spending breakdown, bank balances, rolling cash flow projection is what a founder would open on a Monday morning.

Skills demonstrated
Dashboard configuration Cash flow monitoring Spend visibility
11

Caveats & known issues

A bookkeeping portfolio's credibility depends on disclosing what's unresolved and not on pretending it doesn't exist.

Ksh0.10 reconciliation variance left unadjusted

Reconciled cycle closed with a ten-cent difference, left as is per standard materiality practice rather than forced to zero.

Resolved by policy immaterial

12
Wrapping up

Takeaways

What this build was actually designed show.

The project is not about a perfect ledger instead it shows how I work through a real one by building the structure with intent, automating the repetitive parts, tracing numbers before trusting them and saying what's still open.

Skills demonstrated

  • Chart of accounts design
  • Inventory & three way matchimg (PO → receipt → bill)
  • Invoicing, Accounts Receivable monitoring, and deposit tracking
  • Expense automation design (Zapier)
  • Bank feed rule configuration & monthly reconciliation
  • Accrual basis Profit&Loss and Balance Sheet preparation
  • Statement verification
  • Transparent disclosure of data quality gaps

Tools used

  • QuickBooks Online Plus
  • Expensify
  • Zapier
  • Google Sheets
  • Excel
